steps.title: Analyze the parabola using vertex form
steps.content: The parabola $y = -3(x - 23)^2 + 20$ is in vertex form $y = a(x - h)^2 + k$ with vertex $(23, 20)$. Since $a = -3 < 0$, the parabola opens downward. This means the maximum $y$-value on the parabola is $20$.

steps.title: Compare the horizontal line to the parabola
steps.content: The horizontal line $y = 23$ is above the parabola's maximum value of $20$ (since $23 > 20$). A horizontal line positioned above a downward-opening parabola cannot intersect it. Therefore, there are zero intersection points. That matches choice D — D is correct.
